## Prior art

<!--
Has another library solved this (Cerebras, torchao, rigl-torch, etc.)?
Has a paper proposed it? Our rule is "borrow, don't reinvent" — if
there's a canonical approach, we'd rather adapt it than invent our
own. Links welcome.
-->

## Why this belongs in SparseLab specifically

<!--
SparseLab is scoped narrowly: actually-sparse training from scratch,
CPU-first, PyTorch-native. If your feature is outside that scope
(e.g., post-training pruning, GPU-dense optimization, a standalone
tool not tied to sparse training) it may be better as a separate
project. Help us see the fit.
-->
